title: Pact System — voluntary run curses for rewards
problem: Run Stakes gave players a positive micro-goal, but veteran players have no way to opt into additional challenge for commensurate reward. Every run is equally punishing (or safe). Players who have cleared the game seek risk/reward levers — there is nothing to spend that confidence on.

lineage.spawnReason: new system: voluntary run-modifier layer on ClassSelectScreen alongside stakes — separate from stakes because pacts are challenge-focused (curse+reward) rather than goal-focused

analysis: The Pact system adds a second pre-run declaration alongside Stakes. Four pacts are available, each with a specific curse (always active, no escape) and a reward (automatic, unconditional). The design constraint: every pact must have a curse that is genuinely threatening at all skill levels and a reward that is always useful regardless of build. Blood (+35% damage taken / +50% MF) targets loot-hunters willing to die faster. Suffering (all monsters Cursed / +30% XP) targets leveling players who can manage the defense halving. Frailty (no potions drop / all drops auto-identified) targets experienced players with stash depth who don't need potions. Exhaustion (start each floor at 50% stamina / +25% MF) targets players who manage resources carefully. Pacts persist across runs like Stakes — they are the player's declared risk preference for a season of play.
implementation: gameStore.ts: PactId = 'blood'|'suffering'|'frailty'|'exhaustion'. PactDef interface {id, label, curse, reward, icon}. PACT_DEFS array (4 entries) exported. activePact: PactId|null in GameState. setPact(id: PactId|null) action. activePact intentionally NOT reset in startRun (same pattern as activeStake). ClassSelectScreen.tsx: pact picker row after stake hint, before DESCEND. 4 chips, red-themed, toggle on/off. pactHintBox shows curse (red) and reward (green) of selected pact. combatStore.ts: pactBloodPenalty=-0.35 added to incomingDamageReduction on attack+skill actions (negative = extra damage taken). handleVictory: mfBonus += (blood:50)+(exhaustion:25); xpMult=1.3 if suffering; noPotions=pact==='frailty' passed to rollLoot; frailty post-process auto-identifies all drops. engine/monsters.ts/spawnMonster: if activePact==='suffering' && !affixes.includes('cursed') → force-add 'cursed' affix and append '(Cursed)' to displayName. store/gridStore.ts/initFloor: if activePact==='exhaustion' → startStamina=Math.round(STAMINA_MAX*0.5) instead of STAMINA_MAX.

decisions.decision: Blood pact curse uses negative incomingDamageReduction (-0.35) rather than a separate damage multiplier
decisions.rationale: The incomingDamageReduction field already flows through the entire damage mitigation chain (defense reduction, block, mana shield). Using -0.35 means the pact curse compounds with all existing defenses without any special-casing: Battle Cry at 0.35 exactly cancels Blood pact at -0.35, creating a meaningful interaction. A separate multiplier would require new branching in executeMonsterAttack.
alternativesConsidered: Separate bloodPactMultiplier field in CombatAction — more explicit but requires new engine branch
Apply in the damage formula directly — breaks the clean engine/store boundary
decisions.recommendation: Negative incomingDamageReduction. Stack-agnostic: works in any runtime that uses applyCombatAction.

decisions.decision: Pacts persist across startRun — not reset per run
decisions.rationale: A pact represents the player's current season of play, not a single run bet. Resetting would force re-selection on every class select, adding friction. The pact's curse is always active once set — there is no session without it. Players who want to deactivate tap the chip to toggle off.
alternativesConsidered: Reset pact per run — adds friction, removes the 'curse build' identity for repeat players
decisions.recommendation: Persist activePact across startRun. Only toggled explicitly by setPact(null). Same pattern as activeStake.

decisions.decision: Frailty auto-identify is a post-process over loot, not a rollLoot flag on each item
decisions.rationale: Items are generated by rollLoot with identified:false by default. A post-process pass (loot.forEach(i => i.identified=true)) applies identification cleanly without threading 'should I identify this?' logic through the item generators. frailty and the unique study bonus both use this pattern and compose correctly.
alternativesConsidered: identified: true in generateItem when frailty active — couples item generation to store state, breaks engine purity
decisions.recommendation: Post-process in handleVictory after rollLoot returns. Engine remains pure.

successCriteria: Blood: player takes 35% more damage per hit (verifiable vs. base run at same floor). Suffering: all monsters display '(Cursed)' suffix, XP log shows '×1.3' multiplier. Frailty: no HP/mana/stamina potions in any loot drop; all items land identified. Exhaustion: stamina starts at 50 on each floor entry. Pact persists across runs. Pact chip toggles off when tapped again. TypeScript 0 errors.
knownLimitations: Multiple pacts cannot be active simultaneously — only one at a time. A 'stacked pacts' mode is a future hard-mode option.
Pact reward for Blood and Exhaustion (MF bonus) has no UI feedback — the player cannot see their current effective magic find %
Exhaustion pact starts each floor at 50% stamina but combat-victory stamina drain can push it to 0 mid-floor with no floor-entry recovery
userGuide:
## The Pact System

Before descending, you may bind yourself to a **Pact** — a voluntary curse that transforms the entire run in exchange for a permanent reward. Pacts are toggled in the pre-run footer below the Hunt picker.

Tap a pact chip to activate it. Tap again to deactivate. Your pact persists between runs — it's your current season pledge.

| Pact | Curse | Reward |
|---|---|---|
| ⚔ BLOOD | +35% damage taken from all sources | +50% magic find |
| ☠ SUFFERING | All monsters are Cursed (your defense is halved vs. all enemies) | +30% XP per kill |
| ◈ FRAILTY | No potions of any kind drop from enemies | All item drops are auto-identified |
| ⚡ EXHAUSTION | You start each floor at 50% stamina | +25% magic find |

### Interactions to know
- **Blood + Battle Cry (Warrior skill)**: Battle Cry reduces incoming damage by 35% for 2 rounds — this exactly cancels the Blood pact damage penalty for those rounds.
- **Suffering + Cursed defense halving**: Cursed monsters already halve your defense in vanilla combat. Under Suffering pact, every monster halves your defense, effectively removing armor as a defensive layer. Blocking and dodge skills become more important.
- **Frailty + stash depth**: Frailty is the veteran's pact — if your stash has enough pre-stocked potions, the curse is almost irrelevant. The reward (auto-identify) saves significant time when hunting rare and unique items.
- **Exhaustion + stamina management**: At 50% stamina, your block chance is already unaffected (penalty triggers below 25%). Two heavy combats in a row can push stamina to 0 mid-floor — stamina potions (from pre-stocked stash) become critical under this pact.
componentFile: src/store/gameStore.ts (PactId, PACT_DEFS, activePact, setPact), src/screens/ClassSelectScreen.tsx, src/store/combatStore.ts (blood penalty, handleVictory mfBonus+noPotions+xpMult), src/engine/monsters.ts (suffering curse injection), src/store/gridStore.ts (exhaustion initFloor)

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition — all four pacts implemented and wired across 5 files

publicInterface.name: PACT_DEFS
publicInterface.description: { id, label, curse, reward, icon } — 4 entries, exported from gameStore for UI consumption

publicInterface.name: GameState.activePact
publicInterface.description: Currently active pact; persists across startRun. null = no pact.

publicInterface.name: setPact
publicInterface.description: Toggle pact selection from ClassSelectScreen. Pass null to deactivate.

codeSnippets.label: Blood pact damage penalty in attackAction
Suffering pact: inject Cursed affix on spawn (monsters.ts)
